text: I registered on the website, tried to order a laptop, entered all the details, but instead of charging me and sending the product, they froze my account, demanding various verification documents.

Amazon locked my account, no idea why, sent an email saying that after completing form, which i did, they would be in contact within 24hrs. After 26 hours I attempted to contact them. No callback service available.

If they see that a Fulfillment center lost my package they should contact the facility who lost my package or send a replacement. This company has working employees that are nothing but thieves and customer service is horrible.

After being a prime member for years, Amazon has randomly decided to close my account this week. They say it's for misuse because I've returned too many items. I have NEVER returned an item to Amazon!
The last 3 orders I have placed did not meet the delivery date. The last order was the wrong item. It was a lesser quality and was less cost.

Whilst connecting a smart TV to the 'in built' Amazon Prime, I was directed to another page which asked me to call 08003213437. A individual answered and started to give me pass codes, then started to ask me about renewing prime for 2 years at a reduced rate. I became very concerned at this.

I recently bought a $300 item that broke just after the return time. I notified the seller and got no help, so I reached out to AMAZON. They refused to help and told me I was stuck with the broken product and now am out $300!!!

I spent about 5 hours on calls to try to clear up some issues with my account - I somehow had 2 accounts, and being charged for both. For those of us who work internationally and have different phone / sim cards - the genius move to phone verification is a NIGHTMARE.

I have had 2 instances of credit card fraud immediately after using Amazon 3rd party providers. A provider doubled my order and charged me $75 twice. They accepted the return of the duplicate but refused to cover the postage.

In my recent experiences with refurbished computers and electronics I received several defective products from Amazon third party sellers. The third party sellers did not respond for refunds or returns. Amazon did NOT stand behind their A to Z Guarantee.

We ordered twice recently from Amazon said free returns and after they sent a broken item would not give us a full refund. Another item spoiled its contents and they would only take feedback not offer any refund.

I recently placed an order from Amazon that their delivery driver caused to be stolen. When I called to complain and get my money back which was almost £50 they refused, despite my CCTV footage showing he did not follow my instructions.

Delivery driver literally threw my package, from waist high onto my concrete porch. I asked her why she threw the package and she said 'there is your package'. I tried to share my experience with Amazon and they say they'll train drivers not to throw packages on a concrete porch.
Absolutely asinine that I HAVE to be home and use this security pass code you've enacted. I HAVE to be home from work when the package is being delivered so I'm losing money because of you.

Amazon keeps charging more $ but you get less customer service, especially shipping time. When I signed up there was guaranteed two day shipping. Now I'm lucky to get one out of ten orders in two days. Sometimes it's weeks.
Last three things I bought from Amazon were counterfeit. A holographic sight. Protein powder and even vitamins were counterfeit.

Amazon 'Limited' my account after I updated my credit card information. They need a picture of my drivers license, a bank statement, credit card statement and so much more. All uploaded to a sketchy cloud service!

Bought two pairs of shorts off an Amazon listed seller for holiday. Wore them once and found out zip was faulty. Tried to get refund but was told I was too late. So much for UK consumer law! Obviously doesn't apply to Amazon sellers.

Constant brush off emails. Anytime you complain they just tell you they are 'investigating' and never do anything. Sellers bribe the Amazon Community managers to alter reviews, and there is no way to report the transgressions.

I got charged for almost a year for three subscriptions that I did not purchase! Then they go back and only give us a refund for one month and said we had to dispute the others with our credit card company.

Charged for an account that had been canceled long ago. But this current charge was for full price. When I finally got a refund, the bank charged a $20 overdraft fee. I was never given advanced notice of this.

I got an offer today to join Amazon prime for FREE for 30 days, I clicked on the offer, nothing happened. I've been trying to redeem this offer for 45 MINUTES via chat with 3 agents, then on the phone for 32 MINUTES and still absolutely ZERO help.
I have been a prime member for 10 years and I am not going to renew my membership. Packages are all running late lately. I received a lot of expired/used item one of which could not be returned.
Amazon delivery service in Central Texas is awful. The driver blames AMAZON GPS. They either can't find my address or they can't read the zip code and deliver to the wrong address in the wrong city.
According to the Amazon website, a 'Used - Like New' item should have all its essential accessories complete. Despite that claim, I got a weather station but its outdoor sensor was missing.
Amazon has really gone downhill. Their customer service has also deteriorated. One book had some kind of very strong smelling sticky stuff all over it that was not from my order.

I purchased 4 black leggings that bled all over my white LV bag and the stains will not come out. All Amazon offered was to give me my money back and for me to keep the defective leggings. What about my bag that your product destroyed!

An order I placed for a costume was originally to be delivered 6/25 is now delayed. This order is for a costume that my granddaughter had to have for curtain call on 6/27 for a musical. Customer service could not help me.

I ordered a part for my RV that ended up being the wrong one, so I returned it. Amazon credited my Amazon account $216 instead of putting it back on the card they took it from. There's no way to get it back from them.
If I could give zero stars, I would. Amazon has dipped so far below satisfaction in their customer service department. No one seems to speak or understand English and it is so frustrating.

Amazon Subscribe and Save changed the last day you can make changes to your delivery by several days without any notice to the customer. This caused me to be charged for a lot of items that I didn't want.

Made purchase! Shows that purchased was made. Then what they did was took the money out of my visa debit card! Doesn't even now show on app that purchase was ever made!!! They can take out the money but refuse to show it.
Never been more disgusted with a company. You send 2 pairs of shorts back in the same packaging that you received them in yet they have only received one pair so someone has stolen this item.
Been waiting now for a month for our patio furniture. It was supposed to come last week in time for my Daughter birthday party. It was cancelled and received today but only half of it.

It's 2 weeks after my item should have been delivered. One agent told me that my item was stolen in transit but assured me that another one would be sent and it will arrive tomorrow. He never called, and the item never arrived.

Customer service assistants don't listen, just regurgitate scripted rubbish. Around 15% of my locker deliveries are late. They tell me to collect the next day after being repeatedly told I'm leaving the area that day.
Amazon was once a decent company but in recent times they have become too big for their customers. Every order that I have placed in the last 6 months has been delayed for no apparent reason.
Made an order, but never received any tracking details after asking four times with no reply. I'm confirmed I'll be refunded and emailed to confirm as well. I never received any update since.
Amazon used to be great but now they have an absolute ton of fake companies selling imitation products. We've been ripped off three times in three months.
Three times I've cancelled prime. Three times 'free trial'. Failed to deliver on the day (the whole day) twice. You are the next stop. Great! No he's gone somewhere else!

I was charged for a product that I did not receive and was reassured multiple times by customer service that I would not be charged for the replacement, but my credit card was charged after spending hours on the phone.
Don't spend any more money on Amazon's same-day delivery promises—you're not gonna get your stuff any quicker.
Delivery driver was too lazy to walk up stairs and threw my parcel at me. Absolutely disgusting unacceptable behaviour!
Can't even place an Amazon order unless you're a Prime member—when you say no, a little round circle spins for hours and you can forget ordering at that point.
Since Amazon began making their own in-house deliveries, in over 12 months every delivery has been late or delivered to the entirely wrong address on my road.
Amazon double charged me and their response was far from swift—I had to rely on my bank to refund me because Amazon failed to accept responsibility and put the phone down on me.
Amazon enables cyber-stalking by not providing a phone number on the website and forcing customers through an automated system that blocks access to humans.
Charged postage and VAT despite spending over £25 and meeting free delivery requirements, and now having trouble cancelling my account.
Amazon has not given me my refund even though they received the returned item over 2 weeks ago, and it's impossible to find how to contact a real human being.
They locked me out of my account and asked for bank statements with confidential info which they have no right to. Abuse of power. I am out.
My poster arrived damaged and I requested a return, but when I tried to leave a negative review with pictures to warn others, Amazon rejected it.
They charged me £95 for a Prime subscription I never requested and the items weren't even ordered because there wasn't enough money in my account.
Amazon introduced a separate £2.99 monthly payment for Prime Video to remove ads, breaking the contract that stated advert-free movies and TV series.
Colleague confirmed refund was processed with email, but when I checked I was told this was misinformation and I wasn't getting a refund.
Amazon driver KN70SYT failed to give way exiting Mayflower Street, nearly taking out my wife and me and two elderly pedestrians, then blocked the pavement.
A quarter of the time either someone else's package gets delivered to my door or my package gets lost even though it says 'delivered,' and you only have 30 days to complain.
My damaged item was returned to vendor and I spent 2 hours on the phone transferred to 5 different agents but the issue was never resolved.
I cancelled my order but they charged me for it and sent it anyway, and I can't even contact customer support to get a solution.
Amazon Prime now forces you to watch ADVERTS on a service you paid not to have ads—this is a total scam and I'm stopping my Prime.
It took 3 years of fighting with support to find out why they suspended my account, but they couldn't even find out themselves because they keep redirecting between different geo-Amazons.
Been waiting over 3 weeks for two iPhone 15s with express delivery, Amazon keeps refusing to accept 7 incident reports, and seems to be delaying assistance until refund cut-off.
I have been hacked twice and spent an hour on the phone trying to get into Prime Video, then discovered another email listed on my account. I've canceled Prime and will close my Amazon account.
The Amazon driver delivering my partner's parcel was smoking a cigarette with it hanging out of his mouth and just said 'here' rudely.
Amazon deleted my account without explanation after my order never arrived—tech support can't help or get my money back, and I don't understand how this is possible.
Amazon's app blocks you from reporting missing items upon delivery—I received 4 of 5 items but the app reports all 5 as received and won't let me report the missing one.
Amazon is allowing sellers to sell stolen electronics with parts stolen and put into new casing—I received a broken tablet instead of a laptop.
Amazon erroneously charged me £49.93, I went overdrawn and had to ask my council for help for food, and they refused to play fair or honestly.
You are very crafty in the way you ask people to cancel Prime—the button kept saying fewer days so I thought it was already cancelled, but you never sent an email about the charge.
THOUSANDS MISSING! One of their employees stole my card number. STAY CLEAR.
Been lied to by customer service agents who claim delivery was handed to me and I have a printout and witness, but I'm speaking to a wall. Their MO is lie, cry and deny.
My account was disabled for buying with my own card—support said they reviewed it but couldn't remove the hold, and after I sent necessary documents they closed my account.
Every other order there's an issue: I've received used items, poorly packaged items, empty packages altogether. Amazon is not a safe place to shop, totally unreliable.
I tried calling 5 times this morning and been cut off every time. You do not even deserve 1 star. Rubbish service.
In the last 30 days I ordered 15 items from Amazon, paid for Prime, and only 1 arrived on time—when they advertise 3-8hr delivery or next day, I expect them to meet that deadline.
Amazon sent me a beach chair instead of a printer and refuses to honor the original price or investigate the fraud—they want me to repurchase at $700 more.
It's an absolute disgrace that Amazon Prime now shows ads, and for an extra charge they'll remove them—just another ploy to get more money from loyal customers.
I experienced racial abuse by an Amazon driver and Amazon never wanted police involved, but the driver is still working for them.
I ordered an ice fishing hut and got a folding chair—trying to return was a nightmare because customer service couldn't understand me and continually hung up the phone.
Each time you follow up on a case, you get a template-based answer from a different agent filled with empty words and little substance. I've actually stopped using Amazon.
Amazon fraudulently takes money for a supposed subscription linked to an old account I can't access, yet they're quick to debit but won't cancel or allow me access.
Amazon routes packages via USPS which falsely reports attempted deliveries to force customers to go to the post office and stand in line for packages they made no attempt to deliver.
Read all the very small print on Amazon emails about free facilities—I'm a vulnerable pensioner and full-time carer who fell for this trap.
After being a loyal customer for 12 years, Amazon banned my account for no reason and when I contacted customer service, the lady couldn't understand what I was saying and hung up.
I ordered Chloe Sunglasses before Christmas in USA and was sent cheap trousers instead—Amazon told me to send back the trousers and a refund would be issued, but refused the refund.
I ordered Hoverboards for my grandchildren that had already been shipped out and recalled for safety reasons—I've exhausted all remedies and will be taking Amazon to small claims court.
Amazon prices are overly inflated—I purchased a 3D Printer with a $150 coupon but found the same printer elsewhere for $180 less.
During December my items disappeared with a notification 'Left at Postage Box' even though I don't have a post box. Do NOT recommend—go somewhere else and be looked after.
My ring doorbell footage shows the Amazon delivery driver didn't even ring the bell or give a toss about my package—absolutely unacceptable.
Amazon allows fraudulent sellers with no recourse—an ink cartridge advertised with 24 months guarantee, but there's no way to reach the seller or get help from Amazon.

If I could score zero I would. Whilst at home waiting for the delivery of a Christmas gift (bottle of alcohol), I was watching the Tracking. 2 stops away, when I looked again it said delivery attempted but nobody in to sign but lights are on in the house.
The Amazon driver took a photo of the delivered parcel on the doorstep and then was caught walking away with the parcel, which was a £25 gift voucher!

Bought a new unlocked tcl smart phone from amazon, received it, tried to set it up and the carrier said it was locked. Amazon said the wrong phone had been sent and a replacement wouldn't arrive until 2 weeks later.

My friend sent a Christmas present through Amazon. They delivered it and left it in full view on my front door step, so that made a nice present for the thief who took it. Amazon is a great service for thieves.
I canceled an order within 5 minutes of placing it, but the order would not cancel and it was shipped anyway despite multiple contacts with Amazon.
I ordered an e-gift card for US$100 to be sent to a family member in the USA and was charged 95 euros, but the e-gift card was not delivered to the intended recipient.
My Fiji bottles arrived with holes in the bottom, leaking into the box. I sent several images showing the damage, but received a response saying my image did not show damage.
Delivery was left outside in pouring rain on the floor, soaking wet and flat, despite a clear note in delivery details to deliver to a specific neighbour and a doorbell sign.
Different sizes of children's Advil change the order from infant to child, and I ended up with child medicine instead of infant by mistake.
When returning multiple items in one parcel, you will not be refunded for all of them - I was initially refunded but then re-charged for individual items months later.
Amazon said they had delivered 3 items to my house but only delivered 2. I can't get anywhere with their complaints system as the bots call me a liar when I say it's not been delivered.
Beware Amazon is now asking for your ID to be able to return an item. They say it is for verification purposes, but their bots can't explain what exactly they are verifying.
Chat agents have lied to me on a number of occasions, promising my order is on its way when knowing full well it isn't.
The Customer service was horrifying - I was on the call for over an hour and spent 10 minutes just trying to dictate my email address. They can't even understand English.
The item was not delivered yet the courier said it was handed to the recipient, this was a lie. Instead of refunding me, Amazon made me wait 2 weeks.
For 2 years I've had to update my payment first - can't pay with my IBAN, payments get declined a lot, orders get delayed.
The delivery driver can be seen on my ring doorbell reading the notice saying not to leave parcels in the bin, then lifts the bin lid and drops my parcel in to my bin.
I wasn't in when my parcel arrived. The delivery driver can be seen on my ring doorbell reading the notice on my bin which says not to leave parcels in the bin, then drops my parcel into my bin.
Delivery driver stole my parcel. Marked it as delivered and I was in the house watching the tracking, but he did not show up.
Three orders placed with next day delivery - I waited in all day and no delivery. Now being told it will be Monday/Tuesday next week. Why we pay for prime membership is beyond me.
Ordered a phone case for my son 1 month ago. No delivery. No response from my email requesting a refund. So disappointing that they don't have the decency to reply.
I watched the tracking and saw the driver was approaching my address, then got an email saying a problem had occurred and I wouldn't be getting my delivery.
Recently, sellers have been sending out books that are so dirty they can't be cleaned, and Amazon creates an environment where damaged products are acceptable as long as they give refunds.
Items have been left on my doorstep when it clearly says leave in the parcel box in the garden on the order AND on the doorbell sign. The courier throws the item to the ground and walks off.
Amazon keep taking money from my 16yr old daughter's Revolut account even though she is definitely not making purchases. Revolut froze the card but payment requests from Amazon keep coming in.
THE WORST customer service I've ever came across. I asked a SINGLE question regarding my prime and these agents juggled me around for AN HOUR and I was transferred more than 10 times!

One item said delivered after I raised a query as soon as I received the delivery confirmation, but no actual delivery occurred. The other was delayed and cancelled, and I lost the option to replace items.
It said delivered through letterbox but not mine. When I contacted them and said this is the second time this has happened, their alleged customer service team had no solution.

To say that Amazon are a shocking shower of s**t would be such a huge understatement. I have had NUMEROUS packages 'get lost' and when I've contacted their customer support, they were as much use as a chocolate teapot.
Pathetic customer service - app and website does not seem to be working for little things like updating the address and mobile number.
Ordered an expensive toy for £160 (instead of £250) for Christmas - didn't arrive. Amazon said it's lost, every time I contact customer service I get told to wait 2 weeks.
I bought an item and was incorrectly charged tax of $25.99. Amazon twice promised to refund the tax but will still not make the payment after 4 months.
The policy states 3-5 business days before money is returned to my debit card after cancellation, but on the 3rd day I contacted Amazon and still had not received my refund.

Don't Order Anything More Than You Can Afford to Lose. Ordered a $1,670 computer, returned it in same box/didn't even start it, but Amazon reps said refund was in process and then sent an email that they couldn't find a record of the return.
Rush delivery is a scam. You buy more to make $25 for next day delivery, then when it's out for delivery they say it's running late. This has happened the past three times to me.
Bought table top dishwasher from international seller claiming no plumbing required when it clearly does need to be plumbed to a water outlet to work. Made of cheap plastic.
Delivery should be next day and comes 4-5 days later thanks to royal mail. I paid for amazon prime so that royal mail would deliver to me in 5 days.
Returns are turning into a farce. Increasingly not given the option to pack & print my own label and neither the post office nor parcel shops can print the label.

I ordered a baritone ukulele and they said it shipped using UPS but couldn't give me a tracking number. My UPS driver friend said all UPS packages have a tracking number but Amazon insists there was none.

DO NOT RELY ON ITEM TRACKING. The tracking told me my package would arrive by 8pm on the 7th of Sept. With this information, 1 hour before delivery, I bought my snails and shrimp which only survive 3-4 hours in watered bags.
Waiting over a month for my repaired electric tooth brush only to find the new model they sent has 3 modes whereas the brush I ordered had 4 modes, and they also sent the wrong travel case.
They have terrible customer service and loose packages all of the time. They jerk you around for weeks assuring you that the package is delayed when it's been stuck at a facility for days.
I received damaged items in 3 boxes but Amazon only generated 2 shipping labels. They're basically holding over 1000 hostage because they can't process the return without all the boxes.
Frequent false advertising by merchants on their site; verbally abusive carriers; carriers who smash items, steal items, or leave items for others to steal; nasty phone agents.
Phone call customer service is terrible - took about 45 minutes with one rep but no help, then was transferred and ended up in silent mode for another 46 minutes.
USPS destroyed my package and tracking says damaged in transit. Amazon reps REFUSE to refund, nevermind replace or offer ANY type of compensation.
Amazon is now deliberately making returns difficult in order to discourage customers from returning any item. The return location choice is now far away from the customer's address.

I got locked out of an old account and got an email notification that purchases were being made. The password on my account was changed and dozens of purchases were being made and sent to my home address.

Outsourced customer service - hard to understand the rep. They always seem half asleep, ask a specific question and they just repeat themselves. Try returning something - you either take time out of your busy day or pay $7.99 for UPS to pick up.
The video game that I ordered was marked as 'posted through letterbox' which is an outright lie. I believe it was left outside and has now been stolen.
My experience with it was devastating, the laptop turned out to be of poor quality and a total ripoff. I advise against making a computer purchase from the Amazon Renewed Store.

My item got shoved through the front door, I opened the package only to find no item in the box. Tried to call Amazon to complain which of course is impossible as the driver hasn't sent a confirmation email.

Tried to order 3 small items, took well over 2 hours because I used a different card. Now I'm seeing that it will be the latter part of August before I get my stuff with $20 shipping for 2 weeks wait.
Just had their driver force his way into my flat and threatened me. I'm 60 and disabled, and I've tried to contact Amazon to tell them but you just go round in circles.
Yet again another Prime delivery not arriving, lost count now how many times this happens. Just cancelled my Prime account—Amazon constantly breaching contract.
Every single time I get a package delivered from Amazon, it gets delivered to the wrong building or door number. It's ridiculous that they cannot pay attention to the address.
A few years ago I bought one tool through Amazon and the new tool was replaced with an old one. I returned the parcel, but I never got my money back.
I called to change the delivery address and was told to cancel and reorder which would cost £100 more. After cancellation, I had to re-order myself at that increased cost.
Delivery message said 'received by customer' but I was away. The package was left on my doorstep in torrential rain all night in full view of passersby, with jeans getting damp.
The worst part of my experience was getting my money back. They kept it for more than six weeks before discovering that the money had actually been paid to another customer.
When I contacted the seller about the smashed plastic tub, she became abusive. I contacted Amazon and complained but they are not interested in the situation.
Every order gets split up and delivered separately 'for speed and to satisfy the customer'. It's a disgrace in this day where the customer should be trying to protect the environment.
The order was dispatched on July 3rd with delivery between July 24-31st, but on July 8th they write saying it's been canceled because goods are out of stock.
Amazon is not keeping your data secured. I bought some product and few days later a spammer called me asking for a pin—how did he know what I bought and have my email?
Another failure to deliver to my address. My personal beliefs are Amazon are taking orders and taking money but have no intention of delivering items.
As a prime member I'm supposed to have two days delivery, but it took five days. The tracking number changed every time, making it impossible to leave my home for two days.
Amazon has become complete trash with all their counterfeit products and fraudulent sellers. I still have thousands of issued refunds that have never been refunded.
I rejected delivery of an item I never had in my possession, so a refund should have been immediate. Nine days on I am still waiting on confirmation of when I will get my refund.
Amazon used CEVA as their carrier and CEVA stated that Amazon auto-set a delivery date of 6/25/2023, but that was a Sunday and they don't deliver Sundays.
I ordered a tablet on June 13 and still haven't received it! Since I ordered, an Amazon truck has been down my street at least 15 times and could have easily brought my order.
We ordered an 'Amazon Pallets' lucky dip package at £39 promising great value with watches, phones, laptops. The package took weeks to deliver and disappointed completely.
